title: Giovanni Pico della Mirandola
summary: Prodigy, count, disputant, theologian-philosopher, and unstable emblem of Renaissance dignity. The portal treats Pico as a figure whose short life moved between university scholasticism, Florentine Platonism, Hebrew/Kabbalistic study, papal trouble, and late Savonarolan religion.

title: 900 Conclusions
subtitle: 900 Conclusions
summary: Pico's massive thesis system and public debate machine, requiring cluster-level source and doctrine mapping.

title: Abulafia
subtitle: 900 Conclusions
summary: The ecstatic Kabbalist whose materials enter Pico through translation and Christian transformation.

title: Act and Potency
subtitle: Oration, On Being and Unity
summary: Aristotelian vocabulary crucial for reading Pico's anthropology of indeterminate potentiality and metaphysical ascent.

title: Active Intellect
subtitle: 900 Conclusions, Heptaplus
summary: The Arabic and Aristotelian concept central to debates over conjunction, felicity, and human perfection.

title: Al-Farabi
subtitle: 900 Conclusions, Heptaplus
summary: Arabic philosopher relevant to political, intellectual, and felicity traditions behind Pico's source ecology.

title: Anagogy
subtitle: Heptaplus, Oration
summary: The upward interpretive movement by which symbols, Scripture, and hierarchy lead the intellect toward God.

title: Angelology
subtitle: Oration, 900 Conclusions, Heptaplus
summary: The systematic study of angelic hierarchy, intellect, imitation, separate substances, and Kabbalistic angelic orders in Pico.

title: Angelology Taxonomy
subtitle: Do not tag angelology alone
summary: PicoDB now separates angelic imitation, hierarchy, angelic mind, intelligible world, Kabbalistic angels, Arabic intellect, Thomistic separate substances, and grace.

title: Angels
subtitle: Oration, 900 Conclusions, Heptaplus
summary: Created intellectual beings and models of human ascent that connect Dionysius, Aquinas, Proclus, Kabbalah, and Arabic intellect theory.

title: Apology
subtitle: Apology
summary: Pico's defense of condemned theses and the key juridical-theological witness for his dangerous formulations.

title: Apophasis
subtitle: On Being and Unity, Heptaplus
summary: Negative theology and the disciplined recognition that divine reality exceeds names, concepts, and ordinary predication.

title: Aquinas
subtitle: On Being and Unity, 900 Conclusions
summary: The scholastic authority through whom Pico disciplines being, unity, creation, names, angels, and Christian metaphysics.

title: Aristotle
subtitle: 900 Conclusions, On Being and Unity
summary: The philosopher whose concord with Plato Pico repeatedly asserts, tests, and reconfigures across metaphysics and natural philosophy.

title: Ascent
subtitle: Oration, Heptaplus, Commento
summary: The movement from lower life through intellectual and angelic forms toward God, joining ethics, metaphysics, Kabbalah, and prayer.

title: Astral Magic
subtitle: Commento, Disputationes, 900 Conclusions
summary: The field where Ficinian celestial mediation, images, music, spiritus, and Pico's later astrology critique must be carefully separated.

title: Astrology
subtitle: Commento, Disputationes
summary: The science, art, and divinatory practice Pico uses, limits, and attacks across his career.

title: Astrology Taxonomy
subtitle: Do not tag astrology alone
summary: PicoDB now separates natural celestial influence, judicial astrology, astral magic, poetic astrology, Kabbalistic correspondences, biblical cosmology, anti-astrology, and reception.

title: Averroes
subtitle: 900 Conclusions, Heptaplus
summary: Arabic commentator on Aristotle whose theories of intellect and felicity enter Pico's thesis and Heptaplus fields.

title: Avicenna
subtitle: 900 Conclusions, Heptaplus
summary: Arabic philosopher whose metaphysics, psychology, and intellect theory help shape Pico's source network.

title: Beauty
subtitle: Commento
summary: The intelligible and sensible radiance of form that connects love theory, metaphysics, and poetic theology.

title: Being
subtitle: On Being and Unity, 900 Conclusions, Oration
summary: The metaphysical term at the center of Pico's effort to reconcile Plato, Aristotle, Aquinas, and Dionysian transcendence.

title: Benivieni
subtitle: Commento, Pater Noster Commentary
summary: Poet, collaborator, translator, and mediator whose canzone occasions the Commento and whose later activity shapes Pico reception.

title: Bereshit
subtitle: Heptaplus
summary: The Hebrew opening of Genesis and a concentrated locus for Pico's Kabbalistic and Mosaic hermeneutics.

title: Celestial Influence
subtitle: Commento, Disputationes
summary: The disputed natural action of stars and heavens on lower bodies, love, medicine, and events.

title: Chaldean Oracles
subtitle: 900 Conclusions
summary: Ancient theological verses used in Renaissance Platonism and Pico's esoteric source environment.

title: Christian Kabbalah in Pico
subtitle: Mithridates, Hebrew, 900 Conclusions
summary: Pico's Kabbalah depends on translation, Hebrew aspiration, Christian interpretation, and the ambitious integration of Jewish mystical materials into concordist philosophy.

title: Christology
subtitle: Heptaplus, Oration, Apology
summary: The doctrine of Christ as it shapes Pico's anthropology, Kabbalah, Heptaplus, and theological defenses.

title: Commento
subtitle: Commento
summary: Pico's commentary on Benivieni's canzone, a love-metaphysics and anti-Ficinian text with a complex transmission history.

title: Commento Reading Ontology
subtitle: Love, beauty, hierarchy, Ficino
summary: A new ontology layer tells future summaries what to mark in every Commento chapter and stanza.

title: Commento Translation Collation
subtitle: Italian TEI, Stanley, Jayne
summary: A workflow separates controlling primary text, early modern reception, and restricted modern translation witness.

title: Concord
subtitle: 900 Conclusions, On Being and Unity, Oration
summary: Pico's practice of testing agreement among Plato, Aristotle, scholasticism, Kabbalah, magic, and theology without erasing difference.

title: Condemned Theses
subtitle: 900 Conclusions, Apology
summary: The thirteen theses judged suspect or dangerous in the Roman crisis and defended in the Apology.

title: Corpus Control Dashboard
subtitle: What is acquired, controlled, or still missing
summary: A new dashboard distinguishes OCR control witnesses, reception witnesses, public digital leads, manuscript gaps, and related non-Giovanni texts.

title: Correspondence
subtitle: 900 Conclusions, Heptaplus, Commento
summary: The relation linking levels of reality, symbols, names, stars, angels, and human ascent.

title: Creation
subtitle: Heptaplus, On Being and Unity
summary: The metaphysical and biblical problem of how all things proceed from God and are intelligible through hierarchy and Scripture.

title: De amore
subtitle: Commento
summary: Ficino's commentary/treatise on Plato's Symposium and the main comparator for Pico's Commento.

title: Dignity
subtitle: Oration
summary: The famous but often mythologized concept in the Oration that must be read inside ascent, discipline, hierarchy, and the 900 Conclusions.

title: Dionysius
subtitle: Oration, On Being and Unity, Heptaplus
summary: Pseudo-Dionysius as authority for hierarchy, apophasis, symbol, anagogy, and Christian Neoplatonic ascent.

title: Disputationes
subtitle: Disputationes
summary: Pico's late attack on divinatory astrology and a major textual-transmission problem in the posthumous corpus.

title: Divine Names
subtitle: 900 Conclusions, Apology
summary: Names of God as theological, Kabbalistic, magical, and philological objects in Pico's claims.

title: Eurydice
subtitle: Commento
summary: The mythic figure through whom Pico's Commento sharpens death-to-imagination and ascent beyond lower cognition.

title: Eyn-Sof
subtitle: 900 Conclusions
summary: The infinite divine principle in Kabbalah, requiring strict source control before use in Pico interpretation.

title: Felicity
subtitle: Heptaplus, 900 Conclusions
summary: The goal of intellectual and spiritual life, debated through Aristotelian, Arabic, Christian, and biblical frameworks.

title: Ficinian Love Platonism
subtitle: Direct, mediated, and contested
summary: Jayne helps PicoDB distinguish Ficino as translator, Ficino as source of love Platonism, and Pico as a mediator/corrector.

title: Ficino
subtitle: Commento, On Being and Unity, Heptaplus
summary: Marsilio Ficino as translator, mentor, co-Platonist, love theorist, and target of Pico's correction.

title: Forty-Nine Gates
subtitle: Heptaplus
summary: The Kabbalistic structure of understanding that shapes Pico's final Heptaplus architecture.

title: Four Worlds
subtitle: Heptaplus
summary: Pico's ordering of angelic, celestial, elemental, and human worlds, especially in Heptaplus interpretation.

title: Free Will
subtitle: Oration, Apology, Disputationes
summary: Pico's account of human self-formation, not as modern autonomy but as metaphysical potentiality within a created hierarchy.

title: Gematria
subtitle: 900 Conclusions
summary: Letter-number interpretation that supports some Kabbalistic and symbolic arguments in Pico's source field.

title: Genesis
subtitle: Heptaplus
summary: The biblical text Pico reads as cosmology, anthropology, angelology, and hidden philosophy in the Heptaplus.

title: Gersonides
subtitle: Heptaplus
summary: Jewish philosopher and exegete relevant to Genesis, intellect, and biblical hermeneutics in Pico's Heptaplus context.

title: Gianfrancesco Pico
subtitle: Biography, Disputationes
summary: Pico's nephew, editor, biographer, and possible mediator of posthumous textual and religious framing.

title: Hebrew
subtitle: 900 Conclusions, Heptaplus
summary: The source language and symbolic medium through which Pico's Kabbalah, divine names, and philology must be controlled.

title: Heptaplus
subtitle: Heptaplus
summary: Pico's sevenfold Genesis commentary and the central text for biblical hermeneutics, cosmic order, and post-crisis recoding.

title: Heresy
subtitle: 900 Conclusions, Apology
summary: The juridical-theological danger field around the 900 Conclusions, Apology, magic, Kabbalah, and doctrinal language.

title: Hermes Trismegistus
subtitle: 900 Conclusions, Oration
summary: The ancient-theology figure whose Hermetic authority helps Renaissance thinkers imagine a prisca sapientia before Greek philosophy.

title: Hierarchy
subtitle: Oration, Heptaplus
summary: Ordered levels of being, knowledge, angels, worlds, and symbols across Pico's metaphysics and exegesis.

title: Iamblichus
subtitle: 900 Conclusions
summary: Neoplatonist associated with theurgy, hierarchy, and ancient wisdom in the 900 Conclusions source field.

title: Ibn Bajja
subtitle: Heptaplus
summary: Arabic philosopher tied to intellect, solitude, and felicity debates relevant to Pico's Heptaplus source field.

title: Idea of Man
subtitle: Oration, Heptaplus
summary: Allen's key term for Pico's anthropology, where the human being's indeterminacy and perfection become Christological in the Heptaplus.

title: Ideas
subtitle: Commento, Oration, Heptaplus
summary: The divine or intelligible exemplars through which Pico thinks form, beauty, creation, and the Idea of Man.

title: Imagination
subtitle: Commento, Disputationes
summary: A faculty involved in love, ascent, error, images, magic, and the boundary between intellect and body.

title: Indeterminacy
subtitle: Oration
summary: The condition of Adam as unfixed image and metaphysical potential, central to non-modern readings of the Oration.

title: Intellect
subtitle: 900 Conclusions, Heptaplus
summary: The cognitive and ontological principle linking human knowing, angelic mind, Arabic philosophy, and felicity.

title: Jubilee
subtitle: Heptaplus
summary: A symbolic and Kabbalistic structure in the Heptaplus tied to sevenfold order, completion, and liberation.

title: Kabbalah
subtitle: Oration, 900 Conclusions, Apology
summary: The Jewish mystical and exegetical source field that Pico translates, Christianizes, and uses for theology, magic, and concord.

title: Love
subtitle: Commento, Oration
summary: A central philosophical, cosmological, and theological force in Ficino and Pico, requiring distinction among personal, cosmic, divine, and intellectual registers.

title: Macrocosm
subtitle: Heptaplus, Commento
summary: The great world whose structure is mirrored, interpreted, and perfected through human and Christological order.

title: Magic
subtitle: 900 Conclusions, Apology, Oration
summary: The natural, angelic, Kabbalistic, and theological practice-field that made some of Pico's theses dangerous.

title: Maimonides
subtitle: Heptaplus, 900 Conclusions
summary: Jewish philosopher whose biblical interpretation and negative theology form part of Pico's Jewish philosophical environment.

title: Melancholy
subtitle: Commento, Disputationes
summary: A Renaissance medical and philosophical condition linked to genius, Saturn, imagination, and Ficinian context.

title: Microcosm
subtitle: Oration, Heptaplus
summary: The human being as little world and interpretive key to cosmic order.

title: Mind
subtitle: Commento, 900 Conclusions, Heptaplus
summary: The intelligible level of reality, often called mens or intellectus, where Ideas, angelic mind, and metaphysical mediation become contested.

title: Missing Writings Taxonomy
subtitle: Primary access versus corpus control
summary: PicoDB now separates full primary text, partial primary text, secondary-rich gaps, review-only evidence, early-print needs, manuscript needs, and reception witnesses.

title: Mithridates
subtitle: 900 Conclusions, Apology
summary: Flavius Mithridates, translator and mediator of Hebrew/Kabbalistic materials for Pico.

title: Moses
subtitle: Heptaplus, Oration, 900 Conclusions
summary: The biblical sage and philosopher whose hidden wisdom grounds Pico's claims about Genesis, Kabbalah, and ancient theology.

title: Myth
subtitle: Commento, Heptaplus
summary: The symbolic narrative medium through which Pico interprets Venus, Love, Orpheus, Moses, and ancient theology.

title: Nahmanides
subtitle: Heptaplus
summary: Jewish biblical commentator and Kabbalistic source relevant to Genesis and Heptaplus interpretation.

title: Natural Magic
subtitle: 900 Conclusions, Apology
summary: The licit or contested use of hidden natural powers, correspondences, and celestial sympathies under philosophical discipline.

title: Neoplatonism
subtitle: Oration, 900 Conclusions, Commento
summary: The late antique and Renaissance philosophical tradition through which Pico reads hierarchy, procession, return, intellect, soul, symbol, and ascent.

title: Number
subtitle: 900 Conclusions, Heptaplus
summary: Mathematical, symbolic, Kabbalistic, and Pythagorean principle in Pico's metaphysics and hermeneutics.

title: Oration
subtitle: Oration
summary: The speech prefacing the 900 Conclusions, often misread as a modern manifesto rather than an ascetic, mystical, and disputational program.

title: Orpheus
subtitle: Commento, 900 Conclusions
summary: Ancient poet-theologian whose mythic authority supports poetic theology and Pico's corrections of Ficinian mythography.

title: Orphic Hymns
subtitle: 900 Conclusions, Commento
summary: Poetic-theological materials used in ancient theology, magic, and symbolic interpretation.

title: Participation
subtitle: On Being and Unity, Heptaplus
summary: The metaphysical relation by which created beings receive limited forms of divine or intelligible reality.

title: Penia
subtitle: Commento
summary: The poverty figure in Symposium love mythology, paired with Porus in arguments about desire, lack, and ascent.

title: Pico and Astrology
subtitle: From scientia naturalis to Disputationes
summary: Pico's anti-astrology polemic must be studied as a development from earlier natural philosophy and celestial/Kabbalistic interests into late Florentine anti-divinatory critique.

title: Plato
subtitle: Oration, 900 Conclusions, On Being and Unity
summary: Pico's central ancient authority, read through Ficino, late Neoplatonism, scholastic correction, and the concord project.

title: Plotinus
subtitle: Commento, 900 Conclusions
summary: Founder of Neoplatonism whose One, Intellect, Soul, and ascent structures underlie Ficino and Pico.

title: Poetic Theology
subtitle: Commento, Oration
summary: The use of myth, poetry, and veiled language as vehicles for metaphysical and theological doctrine.

title: Porphyry
subtitle: 900 Conclusions
summary: Neoplatonic author and logical source relevant to predication, hierarchy, and ancient authority.

title: Porus
subtitle: Commento
summary: The resource figure in Symposium love mythology, central to Ficino-Pico debate over love's parentage and metaphysical status.

title: Predication
subtitle: On Being and Unity, 900 Conclusions
summary: The logic of how names such as being, one, good, intellect, and angel apply differently across levels of reality.

title: Prisca Theologia
subtitle: Oration, 900 Conclusions
summary: The ancient theology framework connecting Moses, Hermes, Zoroaster, Orpheus, Plato, and Christian truth.

title: Proclus
subtitle: On Being and Unity, 900 Conclusions
summary: Late Neoplatonist central to hierarchy, the One, henads, and the Ficino-Pico dispute over Plato.

title: Pythagoras
subtitle: 900 Conclusions
summary: Ancient philosopher of number, harmony, and symbolic wisdom in Pico's concordist source field.

title: Recanati
subtitle: 900 Conclusions, Heptaplus
summary: A major Kabbalistic source behind Pico's theses, especially exegetical and angelic materials.

title: Reception
subtitle: Oration, Commento, Biography
summary: The afterlife of Pico's works through editors, translators, readers, myths, national literatures, and modern scholarship.

title: Roman Crisis
subtitle: Oration, 900 Conclusions, Apology
summary: The 1486-1487 failure of Pico's public disputation and the papal/juridical crisis that reshaped his work.

title: Sabbath
subtitle: Heptaplus
summary: The scriptural and symbolic endpoint of creation, rest, jubilee, knowledge, and spiritual ascent in the Heptaplus.

title: Savonarola
subtitle: Letters, Biography
summary: Florentine reformer and late religious presence in Pico's final years and posthumous reception.

title: Sefirot
subtitle: 900 Conclusions
summary: The Kabbalistic emanational structures Pico interprets and Christianizes in the 900 Conclusions and related works.

title: Soul
subtitle: Oration, Commento, 900 Conclusions
summary: The principle of life, motion, mediation, ascent, and embodiment in Pico's anthropology and cosmology.

title: Spiritus
subtitle: Commento, Disputationes
summary: The subtle mediating vehicle in Ficinian and Renaissance physiology, astral medicine, and love theory.

title: Syncretism
subtitle: 900 Conclusions
summary: The modern term often applied to Pico's source-combining project, useful only when tied to debate form, hierarchy, and evidence.

title: Tetragrammaton
subtitle: 900 Conclusions, Apology
summary: The four-letter divine name and a key object of Kabbalistic, Christological, and magical interpretation.

title: Textual Transmission
subtitle: Commento, Disputationes, Opera
summary: The history of drafts, manuscripts, editions, translations, omissions, expurgations, and reception shaping Pico's corpus.

title: The One
subtitle: On Being and Unity, 900 Conclusions
summary: The Neoplatonic first principle whose relation to being marks the dispute between Ficinian-Proclean transcendence and Pico's Christian concord.

title: Transcendentals
subtitle: On Being and Unity
summary: Being, unity, truth, and goodness as convertible or contested concepts in scholastic and Platonic metaphysics.

title: Translation
subtitle: Commento, 900 Conclusions, Heptaplus
summary: The movement among Latin, Italian, Hebrew, Greek, English, and French that mediates Pico's sources and reception.

title: Trinity
subtitle: 900 Conclusions, Apology
summary: The Christian doctrine Pico seeks to confirm, illuminate, or defend through metaphysics and Kabbalistic materials.

title: Unity
subtitle: On Being and Unity, 900 Conclusions
summary: The one, unity, and the metaphysical convertibility problem that exposes Pico's correction of Ficino's Proclean Plato.

title: Venus
subtitle: Commento
summary: The mythological and metaphysical figure through whom Ficino and Pico debate beauty, love, soul, and intelligible origin.

title: World Soul
subtitle: Commento, 900 Conclusions, Heptaplus
summary: A Ficinian and Platonic mediation concept that Pico can use, discipline, or displace through angelic/intellectual and Christian structures.

title: Zoroaster
subtitle: 900 Conclusions, Oration
summary: Prisca-theologia authority and ancient wisdom figure in Pico's esoteric genealogy.

title: PicoDB Writing Audit
subtitle: What the latest passes add
summary: An actionable audit maps Commento, Jayne, translation, and Pugliese findings onto every major essay, summary, profile, and source gap.

title: Aquinas and Dionysius
subtitle: On Being and Unity
summary: A new essay seed frames On Being and Unity as Pico's Christian metaphysical control of Ficinian-Proclean transcendence through Aquinas and Dionysius.

title: Arabic Intellect Tradition
subtitle: Felicitas and ascent
summary: A new essay seed follows Black's path from Averroes, Avicenna, Al-Farabi, Ibn Bajja, and Maimonides into Pico's Heptaplus and 900 Conclusions.

title: Dionysian Anagogy
subtitle: Hierarchy, symbols, ascent
summary: A new essay seed treats Pseudo-Dionysius as a bridge between Platonism, Christian theology, biblical hermeneutics, and Pico's ascent model.

title: Heptaplus After the Oration
subtitle: Post-trial recoding
summary: A new essay seed reads the Heptaplus as Pico's post-Oration recoding of concord, ascent, Kabbalah, and source density through Genesis.

title: Heptaplus and Hermeneutics
subtitle: Black-guided essay draft
summary: The updated Heptaplus essay reads Genesis as a structured anagogical system involving Aquinas, Dionysius, Arabic intellect theory, Jewish exegesis, Kabbalah, and the 900 Conclusions.

title: How We Can Now Read the Commento
subtitle: Controlled text, translation witness, close reading
summary: A new essay plan turns the acquired Commento into work on Ficino, love, poetic theology, astrology, hierarchy, and English reception.

title: Neoplatonists and Arabs
subtitle: Source networks in the 900
summary: A new essay seed maps Proclus, Iamblichus, Pseudo-Dionysius, Averroes, Avicenna, Al-Farabi, and Ibn Bajja as structured source fields in the 900 Conclusions.

title: Pico Biography
subtitle: Scholar-synthesis draft
summary: A new biographical essay treats Pico as a mobile noble scholar whose family privilege, universities, Florentine networks, Kabbalah infrastructure, Roman trial, French detention, and late Savonarolan proximity must be read together.

title: Pico Writings We Still Need
subtitle: Next primary-text acquisitions
summary: A new essay uses Opera reviews to prioritize missing or undercontrolled Pico writings: Basel Opera, Commento, Disputationes, De imaginatione, Carmina, letters, Apologia, Vita/More, and source ecology texts.

title: Pico and Astrology
subtitle: From celestial use to anti-divination
summary: A new synthesis essay reads Pico's astrology developmentally: early celestial and poetic uses, Kabbalistic/magical contexts, late critique of divinatory astrology, and complex reception.

title: Pico's Angels and Angelology
subtitle: Angelic ascent, intellect, hierarchy, and grace
summary: A new synthesis essay collects Pico's angelological materials across the Oration, 900 Conclusions, Heptaplus, Ficino, Dionysius, Aquinas, Proclus, Plotinus, Kabbalah, and Arabic intellect theory.

title: Pico's Dispute with Ficino
subtitle: Longform essay draft
summary: A new longform essay seed frames the Pico-Ficino dispute through the Commento, On Being and Unity, Oration, 900 Conclusions, and Heptaplus.

title: Pico's Kabbalah
subtitle: Longform scholar synthesis
summary: A new longform essay seed synthesizes Wirszubski, Busi, Copenhaver, Farmer, Howlett, Edelheit, Dougherty, and Allen into a working account of Pico's Kabbalah as source tradition, praxis, apologetics, magic, thesis system, and historiographical problem.

title: Pico's Own-Opinion Theses
subtitle: The 900 as Pico's architecture
summary: A new essay seed starts a table-driven project for all theses secundum opinionem propriam, tracking source family, doctrine, transformation, danger, and cross-links.

title: Plato and Aristotle in Pico
subtitle: Concord as controlled instability
summary: A new essay seed treats Pico's concord as a method of testing, correcting, and hierarchizing Aristotle and Plato rather than as easy harmony.

title: Against the Oration-Only Pico
subtitle: Copenhaver, Farmer, Howlett, Walden
summary: The portal's governing historiographical correction: Pico cannot be reduced to a proto-modern Oration on dignity. The Oration must be read as preface, reception object, mystical text, and part of the 900 Conclusions crisis.

title: Angelology Scholar Matrix
subtitle: Black, Wirszubski, Farmer, Allen, Copenhaver
summary: A new matrix assigns guide roles for Pico's angelology: Heptaplus structure, Kabbalistic source control, thesis collation, Ficino-Pico Platonism, and Oration myth correction.

title: Astrology Scholar Matrix
subtitle: Akopyan, Rabin, Rutkin, Vanden Broecke
summary: A new matrix assigns guide roles for Pico's astrology: developmental arc, historiography, early Commento evidence, reform/reception, transmission risk, Ficino relation, and Heptaplus cosmology.

title: Biography Method Matrix
subtitle: How to write Pico's life
summary: The biographical protocol assigns Howlett, Edelheit, Copenhaver, Dougherty/Borghesi, Farmer, Busi, Wirszubski, and Allen distinct roles in controlling life claims.

title: Kabbalah Scholar Matrix
subtitle: Who governs which claims
summary: A new synthesis matrix assigns each major scholar a role in future Kabbalah close readings: source control, praxis, trial danger, thesis clustering, three-pillar concord, scholastic form, corpus discipline, and Platonist boundary control.

title: The Anti-Pico Problem
subtitle: Textual tampering and late doctrine
summary: Farmer's textual-transmission argument warns that late printed Pico, especially the Disputationes, cannot be used doctrinally without checking Gianfrancesco/San Marco risk.

title: Allen's Ficino-Pico Protocol
subtitle: Contentious co-Platonism
summary: Michael J. B. Allen now anchors the portal's treatment of Pico and Ficino: shared Platonism, pointed correction, textual concealment, and technical disputes over One, being, Mind, Soul, Ideas, and the Idea of Man.

title: Brian P. Copenhaver
subtitle: Renaissance philosophy, magic, Pico reception
summary: Copenhaver is a central modern interpreter for this portal because his corpus here ties Pico's Oration to magic, Kabbalah, modern memory, and the scholastic-juridical details of the Roman heresy case.

title: Chaim Wirszubski
subtitle: Pico and Jewish mysticism
summary: Wirszubski clarifies the sources, manuscripts, translations, and Christianizing shape of Pico's Kabbalah, making him essential for any serious portal treatment of Pico's Hebrew and Kabbalistic materials.

title: Copenhaver's Scholarly Values
subtitle: Anti-myth, trial, technical danger
summary: Copenhaver now anchors the portal's resistance to the modern dignity myth and its close attention to the Oration as a whole, the Apology, scholastic Latin, and heresy stakes.

title: Crofton Black
subtitle: Heptaplus and biblical hermeneutics
summary: Black now anchors PicoDB's Heptaplus readings: structure, Genesis, esotericism, Dionysian anagogy, intellect, felicitas, Bereshit, sabbath, and the forty-nine gates.

title: Dougherty's Corpus Standard
subtitle: Genre, editions, collaboration
summary: Dougherty now supplies the corpus-wide discipline for PicoDB: every reading must know its genre, edition status, translation status, reception frame, and specialist needs.

title: Edelheit's Scholastic Trace Method
subtitle: Beyond reductive isms
summary: Edelheit now governs the portal's scholastic-source tagging: Padua, Paris, teachers, school traditions, and the Renaissance scholasticism that keeps Pico from collapsing into a single label.

title: Howlett's Three-Pillar Re-evaluation
subtitle: Aristotle, Plato, Kabbalah
summary: Howlett now shapes readings of Pico as a coherent contested site where Aristotelianism, Platonism, and Jewish Kabbalism converge, succeed, and sometimes expose real differences.

title: Ovanes Akopyan
subtitle: Pico and astrology
summary: Akopyan supplies the main structure for reading Pico's Disputationes, its prehistory, sources, natural philosophy, and pro/contra afterlife.

title: Sophia Howlett
subtitle: Aristotelianism, Kabbalism, Platonism
summary: Howlett re-enters Pico studies from Ficino studies and frames Pico as a contested site whose allure depends on the interplay of exceptionalism, Kabbalah, Aristotelianism, Platonism, and modern scholarly shifts away from an Oration-only Pico.

title: Stephen A. Farmer
subtitle: 900 Theses and syncretic systems
summary: Farmer restores the 900 Conclusions as a massive puzzle and oral-disputational project whose hidden connections structure Pico's Roman ambition.

title: Olga Zorzi Pugliese
subtitle: Benivieni and transmission
summary: Pugliese becomes the guide scholar for Benivieni as collaborator, translator, Commento mediator, and Pater Noster source-gap witness.

title: Sears Jayne
subtitle: Ficino, Pico, and reception
summary: Jayne is now a guide for Ficino's De amore, Pico's Commento translation history, Colet marginalia, and mediated Platonist influence.

title: Biography Source Gaps
subtitle: Letters, Vita, witnesses
summary: A new source-gap packet marks the Borghesi Lettere for letter-by-letter summary and identifies Gianfrancesco's Vita, Capponi 235, the 1496 print tradition, PRDL titles, and the del Medigo letter as acquisition/control targets.

title: Pico's Pater Noster Commentary
subtitle: New acquisition priority
summary: Pugliese adds Pico's Pater Noster commentary and Benivieni's unpublished vernacular translation to the primary-text queue.

title: Angelology Source Map
subtitle: Primary loci and scholarship
summary: A new source map identifies angelological loci in the Oration, 900 Conclusions, Heptaplus, On Being and Unity, Commento, and the major scholarship.

title: Astrology Source Packet
subtitle: Six new astrology documents
summary: The pass 010 source packet records Akopyan, Rabin, Rutkin, Vanden Broecke, and review materials newly copied from Downloads and ingested into PicoDB.

title: Basel Opera OCR Acquired
subtitle: Corpus-control witness
summary: The 1557 Opera Omnia OCR is now in Markdown and the database for search, segmentation, and edition-control work.

title: Black's Heptaplus Structure
subtitle: Seven-part guide to the work
summary: A source packet maps Black's chapter structure and makes it the control frame for future section summaries of Pico's Heptaplus.

title: Commento Full Text Acquired
subtitle: Biblioteca Italiana TEI
summary: The Commento is now a controlled primary text in Markdown and SQLite, sectioned by paratext, books, chapters, and stanza commentary.

title: Jayne Commento Translation Access
subtitle: Restricted modern English witness
summary: Jayne's modern English translation is registered for page-level notes and collation, not full-text transcription.

title: Jayne on Ficino and Pico
subtitle: Mediated influence and love Platonism
summary: A new source packet uses Jayne and reviewers to sharpen Ficino's mediated influence, Pico's Commento, and rival love commentaries.

title: More's Life of Pico Acquired
subtitle: English reception witness
summary: The More/Rigg public-domain Life of Pico volume is now converted and ready for reception-aware biography notes.

title: Opera Reviews Source Packet
subtitle: Six new reviews ingested
summary: The pass 012 source packet records reviews of the Basel Opera Omnia, Speyer's Carmina latina, and the Mirandola congress volumes.

title: Pugliese on Benivieni and Pico
subtitle: Commento mediation and Pater Noster translation
summary: Pugliese strengthens Benivieni's role as Pico's collaborator, Commento mediator, and translator of Pico's Pater Noster commentary.

title: Stanley's Platonick Discourse
subtitle: 1651 English reception witness
summary: Thomas Stanley's early modern English translation of Pico's Commento is now available for collation through the 1914 Gardner reprint.

title: Busi-Wirszubski Kabbalah Protocol
subtitle: Source control plus praxis
summary: PicoDB now has a Kabbalah protocol for Oration, 900 Conclusions, and Heptaplus readings: Wirszubski governs source precision, while Busi governs praxis, correspondences, and Mithridates' formative mediation.

title: Commento Translation Policy
subtitle: Italian TEI controls the translation
summary: A translation protocol keeps our original English rendering distinct from Stanley, Jayne, summaries, and collation notes.

title: Section Summary Style Guide
subtitle: Arguments, references, coverage
summary: PicoDB now has a formal style guide requiring every section summary to reconstruct argument moves, register references, extract claims, mark uncertainties, and name knowledge-product hooks.

title: Summary Coverage Protocol
subtitle: UNREAD to PROMOTED
summary: The reading system now tracks coverage levels from unread catalog entries through source packets, section summaries, claims, cross-links, review, and website promotion.

title: Commento Softening Problem
subtitle: Benivieni, Ficino, and textual mediation
summary: A new transmission note links Pugliese to Allen: Benivieni may have softened Pico's criticism of Ficino's theory of love.

title: Commento Opening Translation
subtitle: Original English from the Italian TEI
summary: PicoDB has begun its own English translation of the Commento from the Italian, starting with the title, Bonacursius dedication, and Benivieni's reader address.

title: 900 Conclusions
subtitle: Concordist disputation program
summary: The 900 Conclusions are the structural engine behind Pico's Roman crisis: a massive public program of philosophical and theological theses whose condemned subset produced the trial archive.

title: 900 Conclusions as a Knowledge System
subtitle: Thesis clusters and hidden connections
summary: The 900 Conclusions should be treated as a debate database of compressed propositions, cross-links, traditions, and edition problems.

title: Disputationes adversus astrologiam divinatricem
subtitle: Anti-astrology and late Savonarolan proximity
summary: The anti-astrology work belongs to Pico's late Florentine years and is central for mapping the divergence from Ficino and the shared terrain with Savonarola.

title: Heptaplus
subtitle: Genesis, creation, biblical hermeneutics
summary: Heptaplus shifts Pico away from a simplified secular dignity myth and toward biblical exegesis, creation theology, and the problem of posthumous textual transmission.

title: Heptaplus: Genesis and the Four Worlds
subtitle: Sevenfold Mosaic interpretation
summary: The Heptaplus reads Genesis as hidden natural and divine philosophy, organized by coherent sevenfold interpretation and a four-world cosmology.

title: On Being and Unity: Ficino Controversy
subtitle: Parmenides, Sophist, One
summary: The On Being and Unity dossier now treats the work as both concordist metaphysics and a polemical correction of Ficino's Proclean Plato.

title: On Being and the One
subtitle: Concord and apophasis
summary: Pico reconciles Plato and Aristotle by distinguishing senses of being and by moving from semantics to Dionysian divine darkness.

title: Oration on the Dignity of Man
subtitle: Roman disputation preface turned modern emblem
summary: The Oration must be treated both as a text attached to the failed 900 Theses debate and as a later object of modern memory, humanist canonization, and debate over dignity, freedom, magic, and Kabbalah.

title: Oration: Indeterminacy and Ascent
subtitle: Opening sequence
summary: The Oration's famous opening is a disciplined program of self-shaping, angelic imitation, philosophical purgation, and ascent to union with God.

title: Oration: Indeterminate Adam
subtitle: Philebus and Idea of Man
summary: Allen's metaphysical reread turns the Oration opening from a modern freedom slogan into a Platonic problem of potentiality, form, whole-in-part logic, and Christological anthropology.
